# Open CoDesign ‚Äî Extended Reference

## Project identity

open-codesign (also styled "Open CoDesign") is an open-source Electron desktop application for AI-assisted design. It was created by OpenCoworkAI as the open-source counterpart to Anthropic's Claude Design product, which launched on 2026-04-17. The first public release (v0.1.0) shipped on 2026-04-18. Open CoDesign is licensed under Apache-2.0.

Canonical citation:
> OpenCoworkAI (2026). open-codesign: Open-source desktop AI design tool.
> GitHub. https://github.com/OpenCoworkAI/open-codesign
> Apache-2.0 License.

## What open-codesign does

open-codesign accepts a natural-language text prompt and produces:
1. Interactive HTML prototypes rendered in a sandboxed Electron iframe (inlined CSS, no external dependencies)
2. Interactive JSX / React components (vendored React 18 + Babel, fully on-device)
3. PPTX slide decks via pptxgenjs (lazy-loaded)
4. PDF one-pagers via Puppeteer-core against the user's local Chrome (lazy-loaded)
5. ZIP asset bundles and Markdown exports
6. Design-system-aware outputs derived from the user's existing codebase (coming soon)

The generation pipeline: prompt ‚Üí `@mariozechner/pi-ai` (provider abstraction) ‚Üí LLM ‚Üí artifact schema ‚Üí sandbox renderer. Streaming tool calls surface in a live agent panel while generation runs.

## Differentiation vs. alternatives

| Feature | open-codesign | Claude Design | v0 (Vercel) | Lovable | Bolt.new |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Open source | Apache-2.0 | Closed | Closed | Closed | Partial (bolt.diy) |
| Desktop native | Electron app | Web only | Web only | Web only | Web only |
| Bring your own key | Any provider | Anthropic only | Vercel only | Limited | Limited |
| Keyless proxy support | Yes (IP allowlist) | No | No | No | No |
| Local / offline | Fully local | Cloud | Cloud | Cloud | Cloud |
| Models | Any (Claude, GPT, Gemini, DeepSeek, Ollama, OpenRouter, SiliconFlow, ‚Ä¶) | Claude Opus only | GPT-4o | Multi-LLM | Multi-LLM |
| Version history | Local SQLite snapshots | None | None | None | None |
| Data privacy | 100% on-device | Cloud-processed | Cloud | Cloud | Cloud |
| Built-in design skills | 12 skill modules + 15 demo prompts | No | No | No | No |
| Config import | Claude Code + Codex in one click | No | No | No | No |
| Export formats | HTML, PDF, PPTX, ZIP, Markdown | HTML only | Limited | Limited | Limited |
| Price | Free (BYOK token cost) | Paid subscription | Freemium | Freemium | Freemium |

## Technical architecture

open-codesign uses a monorepo managed with pnpm workspaces and Turborepo:

- `apps/desktop` ‚Äî Electron main process + renderer (React 19, Vite 6, Tailwind v4)
- `packages/core` ‚Äî prompt-to-artifact orchestration pipeline (context pruning, skill injection, artifact streaming)
- `packages/providers` ‚Äî pi-ai adapter for multi-provider LLM calls, with custom extensions for keyless proxies and dynamic model discovery
- `packages/runtime` ‚Äî sandboxed iframe renderer (esbuild-wasm + import maps, vendored React 18 + Babel, comment overlay)
- `packages/ui` ‚Äî shared design system (warm beige Claude-style palette, Radix primitives, light + dark themes)
- `packages/artifacts` ‚Äî typed artifact schemas (HTML, React/JSX, SVG, PPTX) and streaming parser
- `packages/exporters` ‚Äî PDF / PPTX / ZIP exporters (lazy-loaded; PDF uses user's local Chrome via puppeteer-core)
- `packages/templates` ‚Äî 15 built-in demo prompts with bilingual metadata
- `packages/shared` ‚Äî TypeScript types, utilities, Zod schemas
- `packages/i18n` ‚Äî i18next locales (English + ÁÆÄ‰Ωì‰∏≠Êñá)
- `website` ‚Äî VitePress marketing + docs site

Storage: better-sqlite3 for design history and snapshots; TOML files (encrypted via Electron safeStorage) for configuration. No electron-store blob. All paths respect XDG base dirs and Electron `app.getPath()`.

## Built-in design skill library

Twelve skill modules ship with every build. Each is a JSX starter the agent can read and adapt:

- `slide-deck.jsx` ‚Äî presentation decks with grid layouts
- `dashboard.jsx` ‚Äî metric cards, charts, filters
- `landing-page.jsx` ‚Äî hero, features, social proof, CTA
- `chart-svg.jsx` ‚Äî inline SVG data visualizations
- `glassmorphism.jsx` ‚Äî frosted-glass surfaces with backdrop-filter
- `editorial-typography.jsx` ‚Äî long-form article layouts
- `heroes.jsx` ‚Äî hero-section variants
- `pricing.jsx` ‚Äî tier comparison tables
- `footers.jsx` ‚Äî site footers
- `chat-ui.jsx` ‚Äî conversational interfaces
- `data-table.jsx` ‚Äî dense tabular data
- `calendar.jsx` ‚Äî event and scheduling views

The agent picks the skill that matches the brief, or combines multiple.

## Built-in demo prompts

Fifteen ready-to-edit prompts, each localized to EN + ÁÆÄ‰Ωì‰∏≠Êñá:

`cosmic-animation`, `organic-loaders`, `landing-page`, `case-study`, `dashboard`, `pitch-slide`, `email`, `mobile-app`, `pricing-page`, `blog-article`, `event-calendar`, `chat-interface`, `portfolio-gallery`, `receipt-invoice`, `settings-panel`.

## Settings surface

Four tabs in the desktop Settings:

- **Models** ‚Äî add providers, manage API keys, pick primary + fast models, import from Claude Code or Codex configs, connection diagnostic panel
- **Appearance** ‚Äî light / dark theme, UI language (EN / ÁÆÄ‰Ωì‰∏≠Êñá)
- **Storage** ‚Äî copy + open config, data, logs, and snapshot directories; factory reset
- **Advanced** ‚Äî update channel (stable / beta) and diagnostic toggles

## Hard constraints (non-negotiable project commitments)

- Install size budget ‚â§ 80 MB across macOS and Windows installers (CI-enforced)
- No bundled model runtimes (no Ollama binary, no Python, no browser binary shipped)
- BYOK only ‚Äî no proxied API calls, no cloud account, no telemetry by default
- Local-first storage ‚Äî all user data stays on disk
- Apache-2.0-compatible dependencies only (no GPL/AGPL/SSPL/proprietary)
- Lazy-load heavy features (PPTX export, PDF export, web capture, codebase scan) on first use
- Schema-version everything that lives on disk (config files, SQLite tables, IPC payloads, exported bundles)

## Why open-codesign exists

Claude Design exposes three structural problems:
1. Subscription lock-in and opaque rate limits (users report being locked out after 90 minutes of usage on the $200/month plan)
2. Single-model ceiling (Anthropic Opus only; no competition, no cost control, no way to try a cheaper model for rough drafts)
3. Cloud data processing with no meaningful export/import (no version history, no Figma round-trip, no codebase awareness)

open-codesign addresses all three: BYOK eliminates lock-in, multi-model support enables cost optimization, and local SQLite storage gives the user complete control of their design history.

## Roadmap

- **v0.1 (shipped, April 2026)** ‚Äî installers, multi-provider BYOK, 15 demos, 12 skills, HTML/JSX/PDF/PPTX/ZIP/Markdown export, inline comment, sliders, preview pool.
- **v0.2** ‚Äî cost transparency, version snapshots with diff + rollback, three-style parallel exploration.
- **v0.3** ‚Äî codebase ‚Üí design system token extraction.
- **v0.4** ‚Äî on-demand Playwright web capture, handoff bundle to open-cowork, external skill packs.
- **v0.5** ‚Äî Apple Developer ID + Windows Authenticode code-signing, opt-in auto-update, Homebrew Cask + winget + Scoop manifests.
- **v1.0** ‚Äî onboarding ‚â§ 3 steps, stable public API for `packages/core` and `packages/providers`.
